title: nepeta camphorata var. heldreichii
intro: Nepeta camphorata var. heldreichii, also known as Greek catmint, is a subspecies of the Nepeta camphorata plant, a member of the mint family (Lamiaceae) known for its distinctive, camphor-like fragrance. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antispasmodic effects. The health benefits of Greek catmint include relief from digestive issues, anxiety, and insomnia, as well as its ability to soothe respiratory problems and reduce fever. Nepeta camphorata var. heldreichii can be prepared as a tea, infused oil, or dried herb, making it a versatile and accessible herbal remedy.
